prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "曲腿深蹲" (curtsey squat) exercise, three-quarter front view. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a curtsey squat: right leg standing with right foot flat and right knee bent to roughly 90 degrees; left leg crossed behind and to the right of the right leg with left foot on the ball of the foot and left knee bent toward the floor without touching it (like a deep curtsey); hips and shoulders facing forward, torso upright with neutral spine, hands clasped at chest height, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and gluteus medius of the front (right) leg, plus quadriceps. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: adductor muscles and hamstring group. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the glute muscles of the front leg; "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps; "内收肌群 / 腘绳肌" pointing to the adductor and hamstring area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
